title: Peristylus albidus
intro: Peristylus albidus, also known as the pale ground orchid, is a species of orchid native to the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Peristylus albidus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Peristylus albidus is often prepared as a decoction or a powder, and is used to make tonics and other remedies to promote overall health and well-being.
taxonomy: Peristylus albidus is classified within the family Orchidaceae. This family is commonly referred to as the orchid family. Orchidaceae is a diverse group of flowering plants, including over 30,000 known species. Peristylus albidus is further classified within the subfamily Orchidoideae. The exact genus classification of Peristylus albidus is within the tribe Diurideae.
